rationale: Both land on the right final answer (PR #4792 fixes the #4527 regression, already in the 0.24 rebase). But X's primary root-cause narrative invents an entire mechanism in gpu_ar_model_runner.py (pooler_output/pooler_inter/pooler_client, _FULL_PAYLOAD_INPUT_STAGES, accumulate_full_payload_output, specific line numbers 1818/1808-1816) that is not corroborated anywhere in the ground truth and uses different variable names than the actual quoted diff — this reads as confident fabrication layered on top of a correct conclusion. Y's diagnosis instead mirrors the ground-truth snippet almost verbatim (same file, same `inter_stage_outputs, multimodal_outputs = per_req_payloads, per_req_payloads`, same #4527 attribution), making it far better grounded even though it's less elaborate. X is more thorough (workaround, dismissing red herrings) but the extra detail is largely unverifiable invention, so Y wins on correctness/grounding fidelity to the actual thread.
